skill.name: smc-harness
skill.description: Guides Alpha Harness agents through SMC and ICT market analysis, setup selection, simulated order placement, and trade journaling.
skill.summary: Use SMC and ICT rules to evaluate simulated BTC/USDT trades in Alpha Harness.
skill.author: AaronAbuUsama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and weak cryptography patterns. Human review found markdown command examples, trading terminology, and no shell code, network calls, secrets, malware intent, or prompt injection. The skill should publish with a medium warning because it directs agents to place simulated trading orders through Alpha Harness commands.

medium_findings.title: Agent Commands Can Modify Simulated Trading State
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use Alpha Harness commands such as create-setup, place-order, cancel-order, save-note, and set-alarm. These are not host shell commands, but they can create simulated trades and change harness state, so users should enable the skill only in intended backtesting environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command list and examples clearly show state-changing harness actions. The context also states this is a backtesting simulation, which lowers the security impact.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts map to markdown code fences and inline command names. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, subprocess use, shell expansion, or command construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is plain markdown documentation. The reviewed files contain no executable source file or shell wrapper around these snippets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Trading Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in prose about SMC, ICT, decision making, or market terms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, custom encryption, or password hashing code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are trading methodology markdown, not software cryptography code. The cited lines use ordinary trading or documentation terms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Refer To Market State
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts refer to checking simulated order state and market liquidity concepts. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, credential discovery, or operating system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited state checks are domain-specific harness operations. They do not request host information or system configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing Harness Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown examples include commands that create setups, place orders, cancel orders, save notes, and set alarms in Alpha Harness. This is expected for the skill, but it is a meaningful operational capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit and repeated. They target the simulator, not the host shell.

content.user_title: Analyze SMC Trading Setups in Alpha Harness
content.value_statement: Trading agents need consistent rules for simulated market decisions. This skill provides SMC and ICT workflows for analysis, setup logging, orders, alarms, and review.
actual_capabilities: Defines a wake workflow for Alpha Harness trading agents.
Explains SMC and ICT concepts such as liquidity sweeps, FVGs, OBs, and ChoCH.
Provides trade, watch, note, and no-action decision rules.
Shows setup reasoning examples with entry, stop, target, and risk/reward.
Documents simulated command usage for setups, orders, notes, and alarms.
limitations: Designed for Alpha Harness simulation, not live brokerage execution.
Focuses on BTC/USDT with 4H and 15m analysis.
Does not include executable analysis code or market data connectors.
SMC and ICT methods are discretionary and can produce false signals.

use_cases.title: Run a simulated trading agent
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable wake, analyze, decide, act, and sleep cycle inside Alpha Harness.

use_cases.title: Standardize setup journaling
use_cases.description: Capture structured reasoning for trade, watch, and note decisions during simulation.

use_cases.title: Practice SMC decision rules
use_cases.description: Apply liquidity sweep, FVG, order block, and risk/reward criteria to example market states.

prompt_templates.title: Check current state
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner orientation after an agent wakes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SMC Harness workflow to orient first. Check state, summarize active orders, and decide what analysis is needed next.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a possible setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this BTC/USDT state with the SMC checklist. Decide whether it is a trade, watch, note, or no-action case.

prompt_templates.title: Write setup reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft setup reasoning with HTF bias, trigger, confirmation, entry zone, stop, target, risk/reward, and confluence.

prompt_templates.title: Audit multiple decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of prior simulated trades.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Alpha Harness decisions against the SMC framework. Identify rule violations, missing evidence, and better alarm levels.

output_examples.input: The agent wakes with no active order and BTC/USDT near a sell-side liquidity level.
output_examples.output: The response orients with current state, requests analysis, identifies whether a sweep occurred, and chooses watch until confirmation appears.

output_examples.input: A bullish sweep, ChoCH, and unfilled fair value gap appear with strong risk/reward.
output_examples.output: The response records a high-confidence trade thesis with entry, stop, target, risk/reward, and supporting confluence.

output_examples.input: Market structure is unclear and price sits in the middle of a range.
output_examples.output: The response avoids a forced trade, saves the observation if useful, and sets alarms near meaningful liquidity levels.

best_practices: Use this skill only in an Alpha Harness simulation workspace.
Require clear higher timeframe bias before any trade decision.
Record specific price levels and invalidation logic for every setup.
anti_patterns: Do not use the command examples against a live trading account.
Do not place trades without a created setup and clear reasoning.
Do not treat SMC terminology as deterministic proof of future price movement.

faq.question: Does this skill execute live trades?
faq.answer: No. The documentation is written for Alpha Harness backtesting and simulated order state.

faq.question: Which market does the skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on BTC/USDT in the Alpha Harness simulation environment.

faq.question: What trading method does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Smart Money Concepts and ICT-style analysis, including liquidity sweeps, FVGs, order blocks, and ChoCH.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes, but users should understand that the method is discretionary and requires review of each setup.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains state-changing simulator command examples. They are expected, but users should only run them in the intended harness.

faq.question: Does it include market data code?
faq.answer: No. It provides methodology and command guidance, not executable data ingestion or analysis code.
